The Harvest

"From wild Nilotica trees
to global beauty rituals."

Our Story

Born from Uganda's
ancient shea forests

Deep in the shea belt of Northern Uganda, Vitellaria Nilotica trees grow wild — older, richer, and more oleic than their West African cousins. For generations, women have hand-harvested their nuts and passed down the knowledge of their extraordinary healing power.

At Atunile Organics, we honour that tradition while building a supply chain that meets global cosmetic, food-grade, and pharmaceutical standards. Every batch is traceable. Every transaction is fair. Every jar carries the integrity of the land it came from.

🌳

Wild-Harvested

Zero cultivation. 100% forest-grown Nilotica trees.

👩🏿

Women-Led

Harvested and processed by women in Northern communities.

🔬

Lab Verified

COA-backed, export-ready, and UNBS compliant.

🌍

Global Grade

Meeting EU, US, and Asia cosmetic import standards.

Ingredients

100% Unrefined Pure Organic Shea Butter from Uganda (Vitellaria Nilotica, native to East Africa)

Description

Discover the rare luxury of Vitellaria Nilotica, Uganda's premium shea butter. Native to East Africa and distinct from West African shea, this "Atunile shea butter" is softer, creamier, and richer in oleic acid (50-60%), giving it a smooth, fast-absorbing texture. With a mild scent and pale ivory colour, it provides deep hydration without heaviness.

Packed with Vitamin C (Tocopherol), Vitamin A, Vitamin F (Linoleic & Linolenic Acids), essential fatty acids, calcium, magnesium, iron, and antioxidant phenolic compounds, Vitellaria Nilotica strengthens the skin barrier, locks in moisture, and offers a deeply emollient, antioxidant-rich beauty experience.

Atunile Shea Butter deeply moisturises dry skin, soothes eczema and psoriasis, reduces wrinkles and signs of aging, fades stretch marks and scars, calms skin irritations and inflammation, provides mild UV protection, tames frizz, softens rough patches on elbows, knees, and heels, and repairs cracked skin on hands and feet.

Who We Are

A premium shea butter
company from Uganda's heart

Atunile Organics is a B2B exporter of wild-harvested Vitellaria nilotica shea seed butter from Northern Uganda — geographically exclusive to the Nile basin. We supply cosmetics manufacturers, pharmaceutical ingredient buyers, and food processors globally.

Our Foundation

Purity, sustainability,
and cultural heritage

Atunile Organics is a UNBS-certified, REX-registered B2B exporter of raw unrefined Vitellaria nilotica shea seed butter from Northern Uganda. Our harvest zones span the Northern Region of Uganda — the Nile basin parklands where Nilotica shea grows exclusively. We partner with women-led cooperatives using traditional cold-press extraction methods, and maintain full EUDR geospatial traceability documentation for EU buyers.

Every batch is cold-pressed, solvent-free, and tested to US EAS 967-1:2022 standards. Shipments to the EU qualify for 0% import duty under our REX registration. We offer one of the lowest MOQs in the industry — starting at just 50kg — making us accessible to specialty formulators and indie brands, while maintaining full capacity to fulfil large commercial orders. Available in 25kg, 50kg, and 200kg formats.

Driven by a passion for African beauty and wellness traditions, Atunile Organics is more than a brand — it's a movement that celebrates purity, sustainability, and cultural heritage while delivering the finest organic shea butter to customers worldwide.

We Are Committed To

Four Pillars of Our Mission

Ethical Sourcing

Supporting Ugandan women's cooperatives with fair wages and sustainable farming practices. We go beyond standard market rates to ensure the sustainability of our communities.

Purity & Quality

Delivering 100% organic, unrefined shea butter that retains its natural nutrients and healing properties. Every batch is physically refined using natural clay and heat — zero harmful solvents.

🌱

Eco-Consciousness

Using sustainable harvesting methods that protect Uganda's natural ecosystems. We educate our harvesters on non-invasive collection techniques that protect the health of the Vitellaria Nilotica trees.

🌍

Global Impact

Bridging the gap between African beauty traditions and the modern world by offering high-performance, natural skincare solutions that celebrate the richness of Ugandan botanicals.

Ingredient Education

Eastern or Western Shea Butter?

Shea butter varies significantly depending on its geographic origin. Two primary types dominate the market: West African Shea Butter (Vitellaria paradoxa) and East African Shea Butter (Vitellaria nilotica). Understanding their differences highlights the unique qualities and advantages of Ugandan Shea Butter.

← Back to Blog
Ingredient Education

Eastern or Western Shea Butter?

Shea butter, renowned for its moisturising and healing properties, varies significantly depending on its geographic origin. Two primary types dominate the market: West African Shea Butter (Vitellaria paradoxa) and East African Shea Butter (Vitellaria nilotica), commonly referred to as Ugandan Shea Butter. Understanding their differences highlights the unique qualities and advantages of Ugandan Shea Butter.

Differences Between West African and East African Shea Butter

1. Botanical Variations

  • West African Shea Butter: Derived from the Vitellaria paradoxa species, predominantly found in countries like Ghana, Nigeria, and Burkina Faso.
  • East African (Ugandan) Shea Butter: Sourced from the Vitellaria nilotica species, native to regions along the Nile River, especially Northern Uganda and South Sudan.

2. Texture and Consistency

  • West African: Typically hard and solid at room temperature, requiring warming before application.
  • East African: Softer, creamier, and easily spreadable, melting effortlessly upon skin contact.

3. Scent and Colour

  • West African: Possesses a strong, nutty aroma with a yellowish hue.
  • East African: Features a mild, sweet scent and a lighter, ivory colour.

4. Fatty Acid Composition

The fatty acid profile influences the butter's texture, absorption, and therapeutic properties. Note: Ugandan Shea Butter's elevated oleic acid content contributes to its softer texture and enhanced moisturising properties.

5. Vitamin Content

  • West African: Rich in vitamins A, E, and F, supporting skin health and repair.
  • East African: While also containing these vitamins, it boasts up to 25% more essential fatty acids, enhancing its therapeutic benefits.

The Uniqueness and Superiority of Ugandan Shea Butter

  • Enhanced Absorption: The higher oleic acid content in Ugandan Shea Butter allows for quicker and deeper skin penetration, providing immediate hydration and softness.
  • Softer Texture: Its naturally soft and creamy consistency makes it user-friendly, eliminating the need for melting or extensive preparation before application.
  • Mild Aroma: The subtle scent of Ugandan Shea Butter makes it an excellent base for cosmetic formulations, allowing other fragrances to shine without interference.
  • Rare and Sustainable: The Vitellaria nilotica tree is less widespread than its West African counterpart, making its butter rarer. In Uganda, traditional harvesting methods are employed, ensuring sustainability and supporting local communities.

Historical Significance of Ugandan Shea Butter

The use of shea butter dates back centuries, with records indicating its prominence during Cleopatra's reign in Egypt, where jars of shea butter were used for cosmetic purposes.

In Uganda, the Vitellaria nilotica tree holds cultural and economic importance. Traditionally, its butter served as an edible oil and a base for medicinal ointments. The shea tree's resilience in arid regions has made it invaluable to Ugandan communities, providing both nutritional and therapeutic benefits.

In recent years, there's been a resurgence in the global appreciation for Ugandan Shea Butter, not only for its superior qualities but also for its role in supporting sustainable practices and empowering local women who are primary collectors and processors of the nuts.

Conclusion: While both West and East African shea butters offer remarkable benefits, Ugandan Shea Butter stands out due to its unique composition, texture, and historical significance. Its superior moisturising properties, ease of application, and sustainable production methods make it a valuable addition to natural skincare routines.

← Back to Blog
Skincare Guide

The Power of Eastern Shea Butter: A Natural Remedy for Skin Issues

As a veterinarian, I've always been fascinated by natural remedies not just for animals but for human skin health too. One ingredient that stands out for its incredible healing properties is shea butter, specifically Eastern Shea Butter from Uganda. Unlike its West African counterpart, Ugandan shea butter is softer, creamier, and has a higher oleic acid content, making it deeply moisturising and fast-absorbing. Let's explore how Eastern Shea Butter can help tackle common skin concerns naturally. And if you're looking for a high-quality, unrefined source, Atunile Organics provides 100% pure Eastern Shea Butter sourced directly from Uganda.

1. Deep Hydration for Dry and Flaky Skin

Ugandan shea butter is packed with essential fatty acids and vitamins A and E, making it an excellent moisturiser. Its high oleic acid content allows it to penetrate deeply, providing long lasting hydration. Unlike synthetic lotions that only coat the skin, Eastern Shea Butter nourishes from within, leaving your skin soft, supple, and glowing.

Best use: Apply a small amount to damp skin after a shower to lock in moisture.

2. Soothing Relief for Eczema and Psoriasis

Skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis cause inflammation, itchiness, and dryness. Thanks to its natural anti-inflammatory properties, Eastern Shea Butter can calm irritated skin, reduce redness, and provide relief from itching. Its emollient nature forms a protective barrier, preventing further moisture loss.

Best use: Apply generously to affected areas twice a day, especially after washing. For the best results, use high-quality, unrefined shea butter like the one from Atunile Organics.

3. Healing Acne Without Clogging Pores

Unlike some heavy moisturisers, Eastern Shea Butter is non-comedogenic, meaning it won't clog pores. It has antimicrobial properties that help fight acne-causing bacteria while keeping the skin balanced. Plus, its high vitamin A content supports cell regeneration, helping to fade acne scars over time.

Best use: Apply a pea-sized amount as a moisturiser, or mix with a drop of tea tree oil for added antibacterial benefits.

4. Fading Dark Scars, Dark Marks, and Hyperpigmentation

One of the most powerful benefits of Eastern Shea Butter is its ability to fade dark scars, dark spots, and hyperpigmentation. Thanks to its rich concentrations of vitamins A, C, and E, it promotes cell regeneration, which helps lighten scars and even out skin tone over time. It is also deeply moisturising, preventing post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation from worsening.

Best use: Apply a generous amount of Atunile Organics' Eastern Shea Butter directly to dark spots and scars twice daily.

5. Natural Protection Against UV Damage

While shea butter isn't a substitute for sunscreen, it contains a small amount of natural SPF (around SPF 8) and antioxidants that help protect the skin from UV damage. Regular use can prevent premature aging and sun-induced dark spots.

Best use: Layer under your sunscreen for extra nourishment and protection.

Why Keep It Organic?

Many commercial skincare products contain additives, preservatives, and synthetic fragrances that can irritate sensitive skin. Organic, unrefined Shea Butter retains all its beneficial properties, ensuring you get the purest and most effective version of this natural healer. For those looking for premium quality, ethically sourced shea butter, Atunile Organics offers pure, organic Eastern Shea Butter straight from Uganda. Check them out at atunileorganics.com to experience the full benefits of this incredible skincare ingredient.

Version 1.0 · February 2026

Atunile Organics
Code of Ethics &
Sourcing Charter

Scope: Applicable to all Atunile Organics export operations and supply chain partners.

⚖️

Ethics & Integrity

01

Our Core Commitment

Atunile Organics is dedicated to bridging the gap between the wild-grown Shea parklands of Northern Uganda and the global luxury skincare market. We operate on the principle that Premium Quality must be matched by Premium Ethics.

02

Fair Trade & Economic Empowerment

We go beyond standard market rates to ensure the sustainability of our communities.

  • The Living Wage Principle: We commit to paying a price premium above the standard local market rate to our women-led harvesting cooperatives.
  • Direct Sourcing: We prioritise short supply chains to ensure that the maximum value stays in the hands of the primary producers in Lira, Otuke, and surrounding districts.
  • Financial Inclusion: Where possible, we facilitate early payments or "harvest-start" financing to help our partners manage seasonal cash flow.

03

Labour Standards & Human Rights

  • Zero Tolerance for Child Labour: We strictly prohibit the use of child labour in any part of our collection or processing operations.
  • Safe Working Conditions: We work with our processing partners (mills) to ensure that all workers operate in safe, ventilated environments with appropriate protective gear.
  • Gender Equity: As Shea is "Women's Gold," we ensure that leadership roles in our supply chain cooperatives are held by women.

04

Environmental Stewardship

  • Conservation of the Shea Belt: We educate our harvesters on non-invasive collection techniques that protect the health of the Vitellaria Nilotica trees for future generations.
  • Zero-Chemical Refining: Our export-grade butter is physically refined using natural clay and heat, ensuring no harmful solvents enter the environment or the final product.
  • Biodiversity Protection: We oppose the cutting of shea trees for charcoal and actively support parkland preservation efforts.

05

Transparency & Traceability

  • The Receipt of Origin: We maintain records of every batch, traceable to the specific cooperative and harvest season.
  • Open Communication: We provide our international partners with unedited photos, videos, and impact reports from the field, ensuring they can verify our claims with their own eyes.
  • Third-Party Verification: We actively pursue third-party organic and fair-trade certifications to independently validate our practices.
Global Supply · B2B · Wholesale

Atunile Exports

Atunile Organics is a premium shea butter company focused exclusively on Vitellaria Nilotica. We specialise in Pure Refined 100% Raw Cold-Pressed Nilotica Shea Butter for high end cosmetic brands, food grade, pharmaceuticals and ingredient buyers (US/EU/Asia).

Our shea butter is sourced from Northern Uganda, carefully processed, and handled under strict quality control to protect its natural properties. COA and SDS available upon request.

What We Offer

Export-Grade Nilotica Shea Butter

We work directly with women-led cooperatives and processors to ensure traceability, consistent supply, and fair trade practices. Each batch is produced to meet international export standards and is suitable for cosmetic formulation, private label, and bulk distribution.

Our focus is purity, performance, and reliability for global cosmetic manufacturers seeking authentic African ingredients.

🧴

Cosmetic Grade

Pure refined Nilotica shea for skincare, haircare, and beauty formulations. Meets EU and US cosmetic import standards. COA and SDS available.

🍃

Food Grade

Cold-pressed, unrefined shea suitable for food processing and chocolate manufacturing. Traceable to harvest. Phytosanitary certificates provided.

💊

Pharmaceutical Grade

Meeting pharmaceutical ingredient standards. Full documentation package available including Certificate of Origin via UEPB/UNCCI.
